fields.description:
## Plan

Pure/mechanical module-size split of `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` (855 LOC, over the 800 soft cap; grandfathered in .github/module-size-allowlist.txt + .github/module-size-ceilings.txt with a remedy row in docs/architecture.md). Extract the cohesive `--repair`/A3-remediation cluster into a NEW sibling leaf `src/rebar/_commands/fsck_repair.py`, leaving the read-only diagnostic scan (`_scan`, `_check_snapshot`, `_has_retired_create`, `_branch_mismatch`, `_push_pending` with its own local _git closure, `_transform_json`, `fsck_cli`) in fsck.py.

Relation to the recorded seam: the docs/architecture.md offenders remedy row already prescribes THIS extraction — 'extract the repair cluster (_repair_plan, _repair_ticket, _repair_run, the schedule-disable/enable helpers) into a sibling fsck_repair.py, leaving the diagnostic scan + _transform_json in fsck.py'. The row names the core repair functions illustratively; this plan is consistent with it but MOVES A BROADER SET than the row enumerates: it also relocates the shared support helpers the repair cluster depends on — `_ticket_dirs`, `_resolve_tracker_git_dir`, `_active_snapshots`, `_has_remote`, and the module-level `_git` — into the leaf, then re-imports the two that the staying diagnostic scan also uses (`_ticket_dirs`, `_resolve_tracker_git_dir`) back into fsck.py. Moving the shared helpers to the leaf (rather than leaving them in fsck.py) keeps imports strictly one-way (fsck.py -> leaf; the leaf imports nothing from fsck.py), avoiding a bidirectional import. The row is stale only in that it under-lists the cluster; it is DELETED on graduation regardless.

MOVE to fsck_repair.py (located by name): the orphan-disposition sets `_AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES`/`_HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES`, module-level `_git`, `_resolve_tracker_git_dir`, `_ticket_dirs`, `_active_snapshots`, `_repair_plan`, `_repair_ticket`, `_has_remote`, `_reconciler_pause`/`_reconciler_resume`/`_reconciler_in_flight`, `_repair_run`. The leaf keeps its lazy in-function imports (compact.rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log; engine_dir + rebar_reconciler._advisory_lock) as-is.

Back-compat: fsck.py re-imports (re-exports) the moved names so `fsck.<symbol>` attribute access and `from rebar._commands.fsck import <symbol>` keep resolving. `_resolve_tracker_git_dir` is load-bearing (imported by _store/gitutil.py (function-local, left untouched), _commands/fsck_recover.py, and the tests test_stale_lock_reclaim_write.py / test_event_append_transient_retry.py / test_concurrency_regression.py); `_repair_plan`/`_repair_ticket`/`_AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES`/`_HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES` are re-export-only (test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py) and carry # noqa: F401. No signature/logic changes.

This is a pure/mechanical refactor; E5 testing-plan is not-applicable — the existing fsck test suites are the oracle. (Note: the byte-parity E4 suite once named tests/interfaces/test_e4_fsck.py no longer exists under that path; current fsck coverage lives under tests/interfaces/store/ and tests/unit -k fsck, which the ACs below name.)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] NEW `src/rebar/_commands/fsck_repair.py` exists with 100 <= `wc -l` <= 800, containing exactly the moved repair cluster; it is a LEAF (`grep -c 'from rebar._commands.fsck import' src/rebar/_commands/fsck_repair.py` == 0).
- [ ] `wc -l src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` <= 800 (down from 855), and it re-exports the 5 back-compat names (`_resolve_tracker_git_dir`, `_repair_plan`, `_repair_ticket`, `_AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES`, `_HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES`).
- [ ] fsck.py row removed from BOTH .github/module-size-allowlist.txt and .github/module-size-ceilings.txt (`grep -c '_commands/fsck.py' <file>` == 0 for each), and its offenders remedy row removed from docs/architecture.md (`grep -c '\`_commands/fsck.py\` |' docs/architecture.md` == 0).
- [ ] `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py -q` passes (allowlist == computed over-cap set == ceilings keys).
- [ ] `python -m pytest tests/interfaces/store/test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py tests/interfaces/store/test_fsck_ensures.py tests/interfaces/store/test_fsck_push_pending.py -q` passes.
- [ ] `python -m pytest tests/interfaces/store/test_stale_lock_reclaim_write.py tests/interfaces/store/test_event_append_transient_retry.py tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py -q` passes (exercises the `_resolve_tracker_git_dir` re-export via `from rebar._commands.fsck import ...`).
- [ ] `python -m pytest tests/unit -k fsck -q` passes.
- [ ] `make lint` reports 'All checks passed!' (ruff check + format-check).
